an3.122:1.1: “Monks, there are these three forms of sagacity.
an3.122:1.3: Bodily sagacity, verbal sagacity, & mental sagacity.
an3.122:1.5: There is the case where a monk abstains from taking life, abstains from theft, abstains from uncelibacy.
an3.122:2.2: There is the case where a monk abstains from telling lies, abstains from divisive speech, abstains from harsh speech, abstains from idle chatter.
an3.122:3.2: There is the case where a monk who—with the ending of effluents—enters & remains in the effluent-free awareness-release & discernment-release, having directly known & realized it for himself right in the here & now.
an3.122:3.4: These, monks, are the three forms of sagacity.
an3.122:4.1: A sage in body, a sage in speech,
an3.122:4.2: a sage in mind, without effluent,
an3.122:4.4: is said to have abandoned everything—the All.
